key_insight: The paper introduces a high-order IMEX discontinuous Galerkin solver for fully compressible atmospheric dynamics that handles rotation efficiently via matrix-free operators, adaptive non-conforming meshes, and tailored source-term treatment.
engineering_relevance: This enables more accurate and scalable simulation of rotating atmospheric flows, especially where stiff acoustic dynamics and mesh adaptivity make standard explicit methods inefficient.
method: Second-order additive Runge-Kutta IMEX time integration: implicit for stiff acoustic modes, explicit for advection.
deal.II-based matrix-free DG implementation with distributed-memory parallelism and adaptive non-conforming refinement.
Two nonlinear fixed-point treatments for rotational and gravitational source terms, compared for accuracy and efficiency.
evidence_signal: The approach is validated with convergence studies on rotating inertia-gravity waves and 3D stratified flow over orography on both uniform and adaptive meshes.
watch_item: The rotational source term requires careful discrete formulation; naive discretization can be inconsistent and hurt robustness or efficiency.
paper_title: An IMEX-DG solver with non-conforming mesh refinement for atmospheric dynamics with rotation
